calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition To Release Restriction On Institutional Fund
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel for UC Hastings College of the Law.

The Court stated that it didn't understand the declarations from Mr. Seward at Hastings and that the order listed different counties than the ones set forth in the amended petition. Mr. Rueppel stated that the hearing Examiner wanted to extend the geographical area, and that there were 150 loans/students but not that many from Sacramento County. The Court inquired whether or not Hastings could offer loans at a lower rate since there were 150. Mr. Rueppel stated that they want to stop extending loans, that they want to offer scholarships, and that they wish to increase the geographical area. The Court stated that the request would include the whole Central Valley, that it wouldn't be ameniable, and that there are 71 counties around the Sacramento area. Mr. Rueppel stated that they get a fair number of students from Fresno. In addition, he noted that the definition would then change from "being from Sacramento to having moved there" which would therefore keep connections to the area. At the conclusion of Mr. Rueppel's arguments, the Court took the matter under submission noting that it was not ok with the definition change and that the language should remain as "must be from there" with regard to Sacramento. In addition, the Court expressed concerns with language such as "those just going to college there".

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION Petition To Release Restriction On Institutional Fund
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Continued To Nov-04-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition To Release Restriction On Institutional Fund
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Continued To Oct-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel for UC Hastings College of The Law.

The Petition to Release Restriction of Institutional Fund was continued to October 21, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file an amendment to the petition addressing the issue of why the interest rate is not favorable and geographical description.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
